metadata.title: Incorporation of the Fasting Plasma FFA Concentration into QUICKI Improves Its Association with Insulin Sensitivity in Nonobese Individuals
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp study in healthy nonobese individuals with or without first-degree relatives affected by type 2 diabetes mellitus
metadata.demographics: 57 young, healthy, nonobese subjects (38F/19M), age 19-45 years, white race, BMI <27 kg/m², sedentary lifestyle, no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking. 17 individuals with at least one parent with type 2 diabetes; 40 with no family history of diabetes

evidence.quote: TheJournalofClinicalEndocrinology&Metabolism86(10):4776–4781 PrintedinU.S.A. Copyright©2001byTheEndocrineSociety
GIANLUCA PERSEGHIN, ANDREA CAUMO, MARIANNA CALONI, GIULIO TESTOLIN, AND LIVIO LUZI
we performed a euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp [40mU/(m2·min)] in 57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals with (n=17) or without (n=40) first-degree relatives affected by type2diabetes

Fifty-seven healthy, young, nonobese subjects were recruited at the Istituto Scientifico H San Raffaele. The main criteria for their inclusion in the study were the following: 1) age (19–45 yr); 2) white race; 3) body mass index less than 27 kg/m2; 4) sedentary lifestyle; and 5) no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking.
we performed a euglycemic hyperinsulinemic clamp [40 mU/(m2·min)] in 57 young, healthy, nonobese individuals
3) body mass index less than 27 kg/m2

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - Medical history exclusions
methods_participants.value: no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking

evidence.quote: 5) no history of hypertension, endocrine/metabolic disease, or cigarette smoking
plasma glucose concentration was kept at 5 mmol/liter for 150 min by means of a variable infusion of 20% dextrose infusion
Subjects were studied by means of the euglycemic-hyperinsulinemic clamp to assess insulin sensitivity after a 10-hour overnight fast period
Body weight was stable for at least 6–12 months.

methods_participants.label: Assay - Plasma glucose coefficient of variation - Fasting
methods_participants.value: isocaloric diet and abstain from exercise activity for 3 weeks

evidence.quote: Women were studied between days 3 and 8 of the menstrual cycle
Plasma glucose was measured with a glucose analyzer (Beckman Coulter, Inc., Fullerton, CA)

methods_participants.label: FFA collection tube additive
methods_participants.value: Prechilled tubes containing 0.1% EDTA, immediately placed on ice, centrifuged at 4°C, frozen at -70°C, analyzed within 0-5 working days

evidence.quote: Blood samples for FFA assessment were collected in prechilled tubes containing 0.1% EDTA
FFA determinations were performed as previously described (17) within 0–5 working days
2) white race

methods_participants.label: Assay - Plasma insulin coefficient of variation - Fasting

evidence.quote: CV was 7.2 ± 1.7% and 5.7 ± 0.7%, respectively, in the fasting and clamp conditions

methods_participants.label: Assay - Body composition measurement
methods_participants.value: Dual-energy x-ray absorptiometry with three-compartment regional analysis

evidence.quote: Body composition, with regional three-compartment analysis (arms, trunk, and legs), was performed with a Lunar Corp.-DPX-IQ scanner

narrative_results.statement: S I(clamp), M value, and GIR were inversely associated with fasting FFA

evidence.quote: S I(clamp) (r (cid:1) -0.40, P (cid:7) 0.01), GIR (r (cid:1) -0.58, P (cid:7) 0.0001) were inversely associated with fasting FFA

narrative_results.statement: Fasting plasma glucose and FFA concentrations were significantly increased in offspring of type 2 diabetic parents compared to normal subjects

evidence.quote: Fasting plasma glucose and FFA concentrations were significantly increased in the offspring of type 2 diabetic parents with respect to the normal subjects

narrative_results.statement: The revised QUICKI found a significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in the offspring group (10%)

evidence.quote: the revised QUICKI found a significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in the offspring group (10%)

narrative_results.statement: Simple regression analysis showed that log(I b) and log(FFA b) were significantly associated with IR, whereas log(G b) was not

evidence.quote: Simple regression analysis showed that, when taken separately, log(I b) and log(FFA b) were significantly associated with IR, whereas log(G b) was not

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 classifies as 'Inclusion criteria' while Pass 2 classifies as 'Exclusion criteria'. These are contradictory categorizations of the same medical history criterion.
Pass 1 describes target plasma insulin (350 pmol/liter), Pass 2 describes clamp duration (150 minutes). These are different parameters of the euglycemic-hyperinsulinemic clamp procedure.
Pass 1 describes target plasma glucose (5 mmol/liter), Pass 2 describes blood sampling frequency (every 15 minutes). These are distinct procedural parameters.
Pass 1 describes clamp duration (150 minutes), Pass 2 describes fasting period duration (10 hours overnight). These are different study parameters.
Pass 1 describes blood sampling frequency (15 minutes), Pass 2 describes insulin infusion rate low dose (10 mU/(m2·min)). These are incompatible parameters.

Pass 1 describes FFA analysis timing (0-5 working days), while Pass 2 describes the assay method (as previously described). These are different aspects of FFA measurement and cannot both be correct for the same field.
Pass_1 specifies 10% reduction; pass_2 omits this quantitative detail. Unclear if this is significant omission or rounding difference

structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Whereas HOMA-IR and QUICKI did not detect any significant difference in insulin sensitivity between the two groups, the revised QUICKI found a significant reduction in insulin sensitivity in the offspring group (10%)

semantic_groups.rationale: Both describe offspring with family history of type 2 diabetes, n=17. Pass 2 is more explicit about 'type 2 diabetes' specification.
Both describe participants without family history of type 2 diabetes, n=40. Pass 2 is more explicit about 'type 2 diabetes' specification.
Both specify age range 19-45 years. Pass 2 uses 'criterion' (singular) and 'Age range' while Pass 1 uses 'criteria' (plural) and 'age'. Redundant 'years' unit in Pass 2.
Both specify white race as inclusion criterion. Minor differences in capitalization and singular/plural form.
Both specify BMI <27 kg/m2. Pass 2 spells out 'Body mass index' and 'less than' while Pass 1 uses abbreviation and symbol. Redundant 'kg/m2' unit in Pass 2.
Both specify sedentary lifestyle as inclusion criterion. Negligible wording differences.
Pass 1 classifies as 'Inclusion criteria' while Pass 2 classifies as 'Exclusion criteria'. These are contradictory categorizations of the same medical history criterion.
Both specify insulin infusion rate of 40 mU/(m2·min) in euglycemic-hyperinsulinemic clamp context. Pass 1 calls it 'main study' while Pass 2 calls it 'High dose'. Minor labeling difference.
Pass 1 describes target plasma insulin (350 pmol/liter), Pass 2 describes clamp duration (150 minutes). These are different parameters of the euglycemic-hyperinsulinemic clamp procedure.
Pass 1 describes target plasma glucose (5 mmol/liter), Pass 2 describes blood sampling frequency (every 15 minutes). These are distinct procedural parameters.
Pass 1 describes clamp duration (150 minutes), Pass 2 describes fasting period duration (10 hours overnight). These are different study parameters.
Pass 1 describes blood sampling frequency (15 minutes), Pass 2 describes insulin infusion rate low dose (10 mU/(m2·min)). These are incompatible parameters.
Both describe the fasting period before clamp as 10 hours. Pass 1 is more specific about duration, Pass 2 frames it as an assay method descriptor, but the underlying fact is the same.
Both mention isocaloric diet. Pass 2 provides additional detail about exercise abstinence (3 weeks), while Pass 1 only mentions diet. Same core fact with different scope.
Pass 1 describes exercise abstinence period (3 weeks), but Pass 2 is empty/null. This information only appears in Pass 1.
Both describe weight stability requirement of 6-12 months. Pass 2 frames it as an inclusion criterion while Pass 1 states it as a requirement, but the underlying fact is identical.
Pass 1 contains women study timing information; Pass 2 is empty
Both describe dose-response study subgroup N of 14; minor formatting differences in presentation
Pass 1 contains low dose insulin infusion details; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains low dose target plasma insulin information; Pass 2 is empty
Both describe plasma glucose measurement method using glucose analyzer; Pass 2 adds manufacturer detail (Beckman Coulter) in Pass 1
Both describe plasma insulin measurement using microparticle enzyme immunoassay technology; identical core information
Pass 1 contains FFA collection tube specifications; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains FFA storage temperature information; Pass 2 is empty

Pass 1 describes FFA analysis timing (0-5 working days), while Pass 2 describes the assay method (as previously described). These are different aspects of FFA measurement and cannot both be correct for the same field.
Pass 1 contains clamp steady-state definition period (120-150 minutes) but Pass 2 is empty/null. This is an orphan entry from Pass 1.
Pass 1 contains clamp steady-state CV threshold (<7%) but Pass 2 is empty/null. This is an orphan entry from Pass 1.
Pass 1 contains body composition assessment method information but Pass 2 is empty/null. This is an orphan entry from Pass 1.
Both describe the same fact: total study population N=57. Minor capitalization difference in field name ('total N' vs 'Total N') is negligible.
Identical statements about HOMA-IR and SI(clamp) weak association
Same fact; pass_1 includes 'the' before relationships, negligible difference
Same fact; 'with respect to' vs 'compared to' are equivalent phrasings
Pass_1 specifies 10% reduction; pass_2 omits this quantitative detail. Unclear if this is significant omission or rounding difference
Same fact; 'during the low-dose' vs 'during low-dose' is negligible wording difference
Same core finding about Log(Ib) and Log(FFAb) associations; pass_2 adds context 'in simple regression analysis' which is supplementary detail
Identical statements about FFA incorporation into QUICKI improving association
Identical statements about inverse associations with fasting FFA
Pass 1 is empty while Pass 2 contains a statement about HOMA-IR and GIR relationship significance. No comparison possible.
Pass 1 is empty while Pass 2 contains a statement about regression analysis results. No comparison possible.
